content: If ${ }^n C_{n-r}+3 \cdot{ }^n C_{n-r+1}+3 \cdot{ }^n C_{n-r+2} +{ }^n C_{n-r+3}={ }^x C_r$, then the value of $x$ is
explanation:
$$ \begin{aligned} & \text { }{ }^n C_{n-r}+3 \cdot{ }^n C_{n-r+1}+3 \cdot{ }^n C_{n-r+2}+{ }^n C_{n-r+3}={ }^x C_r \\ & \Rightarrow\left({ }^n C_{n-r}+{ }^n C_{n-r+1}\right)+2 \cdot\left({ }^n C_{n-r+1}+{ }^n C_{n-r+2}\right) +\left({ }^n C_{n-r+2}+{ }^n C_{n-r+3}\right)={ }^n C_r \end{aligned} $$

$$ \begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow{ }^{n+1} C_{n-r+1}+2^{n+1} C_{n-r+2}+{ }^{n+1} C_{n-r+3}={ }^x C_r \\ & {\left[\because{ }^n C_r+{ }^n C_{r-1}={ }^{n+1} C_r\right] } \end{aligned} $$

$$ \begin{aligned} \Rightarrow\left\{{ }^{n+1} C_{n-r+1}+{ }^{n+1} C_{n-r+2}\right\} +\left\{{ }^{n+1} C_{n-r+2}+{ }^{n+1} C_{n-r+3}\right\}={ }^x C_r \end{aligned} $$

$$ \begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow{ }^{n+2} C_{n-r+2}+{ }^{n+2} C_{n-r+3}={ }^x C_r \\ & \Rightarrow{ }^{n+3} C_{n-r+3}={ }^x C_r \end{aligned} $$

$$ \begin{aligned} &\Rightarrow{ }^{n+3} C_r={ }^x C_r\,\,\,\,\,\,\left[\because{ }^{n+3} C_{n-r+3}={ }^{n+3} C_r\right] \end{aligned} $$

$$ \therefore \quad x=n+3 $$

We have, $(1+x)^{41}\left(1-x+x^2\right)^{40}$

$$ \begin{aligned} & =\left\{(1+x)\left(1-x+x^2\right)\right\}^{40}(1+x) \\ & =\left(1+x^3\right)^{40}(1+x) \\ & =\left(1+x^3\right)^{40}+x\left(1+x^3\right)^{40} \end{aligned} $$

âˆ´ Coefficient of $x^{50}$ in $(1+x)^{41}\left(1-x+x^2\right)^{40}$

= Coefficient of $x^{50}$ in $\left(1+x^3\right)^{40}+$ Coefficient of $x^{50}$ in $x\left(1+x^3\right)^{40}$

$=$ Coefficient of $x^{50}$ in $\left(1+x^3\right)^{40}+$ Coefficient of $x^{49}$ in $\left(1+x^3\right)^{40}$

= Coefficient of $x^{50}$ in $\sum_{r=0}^{40}{ }^{40} C_r x^{3 r}+$ Coefficient of $x^{50}$ in $\sum_{r=0}^{40}{ }^{40} C_r x^{3 r}=0+0=0$

content: The coefficient of $ x^{2} $ term in the binomial expansion of $ \left(\frac{1}{3} x^{\frac{1}{2}}+x^{\frac{-1}{4}}\right)^{10} $ is
explanation:
General term of given binomial expansion is

$ \begin{aligned} T_{r+1} & ={ }^{10} C_{r}\left(\frac{1}{3} x^{\frac{1}{2}}\right)^{10-r}\left(x^{-\frac{1}{4}}\right)^{r} \\ & ={ }^{10} C_{r} \times\left(\frac{1}{3}\right)^{10-r} x^{\frac{10-r}{2}-\frac{r}{4}} \end{aligned} $

We have to find coefficient of $ x^{2} $.

$ \begin{aligned} \frac{10-r}{2}-\frac{r}{4} & =2 \Rightarrow r=4 \\ \Rightarrow \quad T_{4+1} & ={ }^{10} C_{4}\left(\frac{1}{3}\right)^{6} x^{2} \\ \therefore \text { Coefficient of } x^{2} & ={ }^{10} C_{4}\left(\frac{1}{3}\right)^{6} \\ & =\frac{10 \times 9 \times 8 \times 7}{4 \times 3 \times 2 \times 1} \times \frac{1}{3^{6}}=\frac{70}{243} \end{aligned} $

content: The sum of the coefficients of all odd degree terms in the expansion of $$\left(x+\sqrt{x^3-1}\right)^5 +\left(x-\sqrt{x^3-1}\right)^5, x>1$$ is
explanation:
We have, $$\left(x+\sqrt{x^3-1}\right)^5+\left(x-\sqrt{x^3-1}\right)^5, x>1$$

$$\begin{array}{r} =x^5+5 x^4 \sqrt{x^3-1}+10 x^3\left(\sqrt{x^3-1}\right)^2+10 x^2\left(\sqrt{x^3-1}\right)^3 \\ +5 x\left(\sqrt{x^3-1}\right)^4+\left(\sqrt{x^3-1}\right)^5+\left(x^5-5 x^2 \sqrt{x^3-1}+\right. \\ 10 x^3\left(\sqrt{x^3-1}\right)^2-10 x^2\left(\sqrt{x^3-1}\right)^3+5 x\left(\sqrt{x^3-1}\right)^4 \\ -\left(\sqrt{x^3-1}\right)^5 \end{array}$$

$$\begin{aligned} & =2\left(x^5+10 x^3\left(x^3-1\right)+5 x\left(x^3-1\right)^2\right) \\ & =2\left(x^5+10 x^6-10 x^3+5 x^7-10 x^4+5 x\right) \end{aligned}$$

Now, the sum of coefficients of all odd degree term is

$$2(1-10+5+5)=2$$

content: $$\sum_\limits{\substack{i, j=0 \\ i \neq j}}^n{ }^n C_i{ }^n C_j$$ is equal to
explanation:
$$\text { Given, } \sum_\limits{\substack{i, j=0 \\ i \neq j}}^n C_i^n C_j$$

$$=\sum_\limits{i, j=0}^n{ }^n C_i^n C_j-\sum_\limits{\substack{i, j=0 \\ i=j}}^n{ }^n C_i^n C_j$$

$$\begin{aligned} & =\left(\sum_{i=0}^n{ }^n C_i\right)\left(\sum_{j=0}^n{ }^n C_j\right)-\sum_{i=0}^n\left({ }^n C_i\right)^2 \\ & =2^n \times 2^n-{ }^{2 n} C_n \end{aligned}$$

$$\left[\because \sum_{i=0}^n{ }^n C_i=2^n \text { and } \sum_{i=0}^n\left({ }^n C_i\right)^2={ }^{2 n} C_n\right]$$

$$=2^{2 n}-{ }^{2 n} C_n$$

content: The number of terms in the expansion of $${(1 + 5\sqrt {2x} )^9} + {(1 - 5\sqrt {2x} )^9}$$ is
If the sum of the coefficients in the expansion of (x + y)n is 1024, then the value of the greatest coefficient in the expansion is
explanation:
Given, sum of the coefficient = 1024

i.e. 2n = 1024 = 210

$$\Rightarrow$$ n = 10

Since, n is even, so greatest coefficient

= nCn/2 = 10C5 = 252

content: The value of $${}^{47}{C_4} + \sum\limits_{r = 1}^5 {{}^{52 - r}{C_3}} $$ is equal to
The coefficient of x8 in the polynomial (x $$-$$ 1) (x $$-$$ 2) ..... (x $$-$$ 10)
